compositional_truth_check.notes: The registry P1-P4 is partially represented. P1 (Yellow Pot) and P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit) are present. P2/P4 (Black Pot) appears to be a single vessel containing a singular, struggling specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss; no evidence of mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss; no evidence of silver guest specimen.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Occupied by a sparse, trailing succulent. Soil appears dry with minimal surface activity.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot_Rabbit: Contains one large, chlorotic/necrotic leaf and one small, healthier green leaf. The rabbit anchor is stable.
plant_audit.P4_Black_Pot_Lower: Contains a single, solitary green leaf emerging from the soil. No other growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dry across all pots; evidence of perlite/vermiculite surface scattering.
biome_observations.debris: White eggshell fragments observed in the lower black pot (P4).

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Minimal change; P3 leaf necrosis is already present.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: Gradual decline in P3 leaf turgor; the necrotic leaf in P3 is darkening.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The necrotic leaf in P3 has become more brittle and translucent. The single leaf in P4 remains static.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical; high probability of total leaf loss on the primary specimen.
visual_health_inference.P4: Fragile; singular growth point indicates low vitality.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P4 (New Intervention).
Total absence of P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) as described in the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of high physiological stress. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from severe chlorosis and necrosis, likely due to improper moisture or light levels. The other pots are largely under-populated compared to the registry, suggesting either a failure to thrive or a misidentification of the initial biome state. The introduction of eggshells in P4 suggests an attempt at nutrient supplementation, though the plant remains in a precarious state.
